articles.title: Israeli, Russian Forces Added To UN Blacklist For Sexual Violence In Conflict Zones
articles.content:
联合国（美联社）——一份记录全球冲突中性暴力问题的联合国年度报告，首次将以色列部队因对待巴勒斯坦被拘留者的方式列入名单。这份审查已持续15年多。以色列否认了这些指控。

这份35页的报告——以色列驻联合国使团周四晚间提前分享，预计周五正式发布——将12个国家的77个政府和非政府组织列入黑名单，这些组织涉嫌在世界各地冲突中实施或对性暴力负有责任。报告称，2025年案件数量较2024年大幅上升。

俄罗斯武装部队和安全部队今年也首次被列入黑名单，因其在乌克兰战争期间对战俘和被拘留平民实施的性暴力。

以色列和俄罗斯都曾在去年的报告中收到联合国秘书长安东尼奥·古特雷斯的警告，称它们可能被列入名单。两国大使都对被列入名单表示愤慨，并猛烈抨击古特雷斯。

俄罗斯常驻联合国代表瓦西里·涅边贾说：“我们将致信秘书长，表示这些都是毫无根据的谎言和指控，再次将俄罗斯描绘成恶棍，他们一贯如此。”他说俄罗斯正在记录并准备一份关于乌克兰如何对待俄罗斯战俘的报告。

以色列常驻联合国代表丹尼·达农在社交媒体上发表声明称：“我们受够了这位联合国秘书长。古特雷斯将以色列与哈马斯、ISIS以及世界上最堕落的恐怖组织列入同一份黑名单。”古特雷斯的第二个五年任期将于12月31日结束。

达农表示，以色列已提供文件、数据和详细回应，对报告中的指控进行了反驳。

报告称，2025年联合国能够记录到以色列及其占领的巴勒斯坦领土上被拘留的巴勒斯坦人遭受性暴力的“模式”，并核实了多起冲突相关性暴力事件，包括作为酷刑形式施加的暴力，受害者包括来自加沙和约旦河西岸的14名男性、7名女性、9名男孩和1名女孩。报告称13起案件发生在2025年，18起发生在2023年和2024年。

报告指出：“违规行为包括强奸，包括使用物体进行的强奸、轮奸、未遂强奸、对生殖器的暴力攻击、针对性射击生殖器、触摸乳房和生殖器、在无明显安全理由情况下进行脱衣和体内搜查、强迫裸体以及强奸威胁。”

报告详细描述了至少9名受害者，其中大部分来自加沙，他们遭到以色列国防军、以色列监狱服务部门、特种部队和警察部队人员的强奸或轮奸，部分案件中受害者多次遭受侵害。

以色列外交部周四表示，已“对这些指控进行了全面、彻底和明确的反驳”。

外交部在X平台上写道：“这一决定再次证明联合国对以色列长期存在的、体制性的敌意。”

报告再次包含了对哈马斯实施性暴力的指控，但表示由于以色列政府继续拒绝联合国进行调查研究所需的进入许可，许多细节无法得到独立核实。

报告称，俄罗斯当局同样一贯拒绝联合国人权调查人员进入，但联合国仍能核实俄罗斯和俄罗斯占领的乌克兰地区发生了310起冲突相关性暴力案件，受害者为战俘和平民被拘留者。报告称大多数受害者为男性。

报告称，乌克兰的人权监督人员记录了31起战俘和平民被拘留者遭受冲突相关性暴力的案件，其中大部分发生在2025年之前。乌克兰未被列入联合国黑名单。
articles.summary:
UNITED NATIONS (AP) — An annual United Nations report documenting sexual violence in conflicts worldwide has included Israeli forces for the first time since the review began more than 15 years ago for their treatment of Palestinian detainees. Israel denies the accusations.

The 35-page report — shared by the Israeli mission to the U.N. late Thursday ahead of its expected release Friday — blacklists 77 government and non-government parties in a dozen countries suspected of committing or being responsible for sexual violence in conflicts around the world. It says the number of cases rose sharply in 2025 from 2024.

Advertisement

Russian armed and security forces were also blacklisted for the first time this year for sexual violence against prisoners of war and civilians detained during the war in Ukraine.

The list for 2025 includes Israel’s armed and security forces as well as Hamas militants, who were previously blacklisted after their attack in Israel on Oct. 7, 2023, which sparked the war in Gaza.

Both Israel and Russia were warned in last year’s report by U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres that they could be put on the list.

Advertisement

The ambassadors of both countries expressed outrage at their inclusion and lashed out at Guterres.

“We will write a letter to the secretary-general saying that these are unsubstantiated lies and alleged things which again portray Russia as a villain, like they do all the time,” Russia’s U.N. Ambassador Vassily Nebenzia said. He said Russia is documenting and preparing a report on how the Ukrainians are treating Russian prisoners of war.

“We are done with this UN Secretary-General,” Danny Danon, the Israeli ambassador to the U.N., said in a statement on social media. “Guterres has put Israel on the same blacklist along with Hamas, ISIS and the most depraved terrorist organizations in the world.” Guterres’ second five-year term ends Dec. 31.

Danon said Israel had provided documents, data and detailed responses to the allegations that are raised in the report.

Advertisement

The report said in 2025 the U.N. was able to document “patterns of sexual violence” against Palestinians detained in Israel and the occupied Palestinian territories, and verified multiple incidents of conflict-related sexual violence, including as a form of torture, inflicted on 14 men, seven women, nine boys and one girl from Gaza and the West Bank. It said 13 cases occurred in 2025 and 18 in 2023 and 2024.

“Violations consisted of rape, including with objects, gang rape, attempted rape, physical violence to the genitals, instances of targeted shooting of the genitals, touching of breasts and genitals, strip and cavity searches conducted without apparent security justification, forced nudity and threats of rape,” the report stated.

It detailed at least nine victims, mostly from Gaza, who were raped or gang raped, in some cases repeatedly, by perpetrators from the Israel Defense Forces and Israel’s prison service, its special forces and police units.

Advertisement

Israel’s foreign ministry said Thursday it has “comprehensively, thoroughly, and unequivocally refuted these allegations.”

“This decision is yet another example of the UN’s long-standing, institutionalized hostility toward Israel,” the foreign ministry wrote on X.

The report again includes accusations of sexual violence by Hamas, but says many of the details could not be independently confirmed because the Israeli government continues to deny the U.N. the access it needs to carry out investigations.

Advertisement

Russian authorities also consistently deny access to U.N. human rights investigators, the report said, but they still were able to verify 310 cases of conflict-related sexual violence in Russia and Russian-occupied areas of Ukraine against prisoners of war and civilian detainees. The vast majority of the victims were men, the report said.

Human rights monitors in Ukraine documented 31 cases of conflict-related sexual violence against prisoners of war and civilian detainees, the majority of which occurred prior to 2025, the report said. Ukraine has not been put on the U.N. blacklist.

Advertisement

Related

Russia

Israel

United Nations

sexual violence in war

Israeli Forces Kill 16 People In Gaza And The West Bank, Medics Say

articles.title: Netanyahu Orders Military To Expand Israeli Control Of Gaza To 70%
articles.content:
耶路撒冷5月28日（路透社）—— 以色列总理本尼·内塔尼亚胡周四表示，他已指示军方进一步控制加沙地带，首先占领该地区70%的区域。此前，以色列已经通过持续的军事行动，有效控制了加不到全面控制的巴勒斯坦领土的一小部分，当地居民已被压缩在沿海地带的一小块区域。

以色列在2023年哈马斯袭击事件后，已有效控制了加沙地带约64%的区域，而该区域在两年来持续的军事打击中已沦为废墟。

在由美国协调的10月停火协议未能阻止以色列的进攻或迫使哈马斯解除武装的情况下，根据该协议，以色列军队本应撤至“黄线”（即军事控制线）以西，该线在军事地图上标明了以色列控制的范围。根据地图，以色列控制了加沙地带约53%的区域，其余由哈马斯控制。

路透社此前曾报道，以色列单方面将地面上标记“黄线”的混凝土块移动到了哈马斯控制的区域更深处。3月份发布的军事地图显示，这一限制区域比原先更大，分析人士称，这一做法将加沙地带约64%的区域划入了限制区域。

内塔尼亚胡多次在公开场合表示，军方已控制加沙地带超过60%的区域。在被占领的约旦河西岸一处定居点会议上，这位以色列领导人表示，他们甚至将控制更多地区。

“我们从50%推进到60%。我的指示是进一步推进，让我们逐步进行，”内塔尼亚胡在约旦河西岸一处定居点会议上表示。

“首先，先从70%开始。我们正在从所有方向向他们（哈马斯）施压。我们将处理残余势力。”

尽管以色列和哈马斯在加沙地带的冲突仍在继续，但以色列仍继续加大对加沙地带的空袭力度，以回应2023年哈马斯袭击事件。加沙地带的缓冲区被视作是为防止未来可能的武装分子袭击而设立的，这起源于2023年10月7日的哈马斯袭击事件。以色列官员将这一区域称为“缓冲区”，认为这是永久驱逐巴勒斯坦人计划的一部分，指出包括国防部长卡茨在内的高级部长曾表示希望鼓励“自愿迁移”出加沙地带。

内塔尼亚胡的指示是在以色列加大对加沙地带的攻击力度的背景下发布的。他指出，周三的袭击事件中，以色列杀死了哈马斯武装派别的头目，这起源于2023年袭击事件的10天后。

加沙地带卫生部门表示，自停火以来，以色列的袭击已造成至少10人死亡，其中包括5名儿童，另有18人受伤。

周三的另一起袭击事件发生时，正值穆斯林节日开斋节，许多加沙地带的居民在节日当天聚集在帐篷营地和被炸毁的建筑中庆祝节日。

一名名叫艾蒂尔·扎伊姆的妇女表示，她和家人在帐篷里庆祝节日时，突然听到一声巨响，他们坐在那里等了一个小时，才从（废墟）中走出，寻找出路。

她表示：“我们是在爆炸声中醒来的，我们坐在那里等了一个小时，才从废墟中出来，寻找出路。”

加沙地带的另一名目击者阿布·阿扎姆说，一个“加沙地带的人完全没有安全感。”

“他可能在街上被击中，也可能在医院被击中，也可能在去市场的路上被击中，”阿布·阿扎姆说。

以色列军方表示，自停火以来，以军的袭击已造成超过900人死亡，而以色列方面表示，同一时期有四名士兵被武装分子击毙。以色列和哈马斯在推进美国提出的加沙地带计划方面仍陷入僵局。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

JERUSALEM, May 28 (Reuters) - Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Thursday he had directed Israel’s military to take more of Gaza, initially by seizing 70% of the Palestinian territory, where the population is already penned into a tiny strip of land along the coast.

Israel effectively controls an estimated 64% of the tiny coastal Strip, bombarded to ruins by Israel’s two-year military assault that followed the 2023 Hamas attack on southern Israel.

Advertisement

Under an October U.S.-brokered truce that has failed to halt Israeli attacks or secure Hamas’ disarmament, Israeli troops were meant to withdraw to a “Yellow Line” demarcating the extent of their control. Marked on military maps, that line put Israel in control of some 53% of Gaza, with Hamas ruling the rest.

Reuters has reported that Israel has unilaterally moved the concrete blocks marking the Yellow Line on the ground deeper into Hamas-controlled territory. Maps issued by the military in March showed an even bigger restricted area that analysts say cordons off around 64% of Gaza’s territory in total.

Advertisement

Netanyahu has repeatedly said in public remarks that the military controls more than 60% of Gaza. Speaking to a conference in a settlement in the occupied West Bank, the Israeli leader said even more of Gaza would be taken.

“We were at fifty, we moved to sixty. My directive is to move to - let’s go step by step,” Netanyahu said on Thursday.

“First of all, seventy. Let’s start with that. We’re pressing them (Hamas) from all sides. We’ll deal with the remnants.”

Advertisement

Israel steps up strikes in Gaza despite truce

Netanyahu describes the territory Israel has seized in Gaza, Syria and Lebanon as “buffer zones” that can stave off potential militant attacks following the October 7, 2023 Hamas-led assault that set off the Gaza war.

Palestinians view Israel’s widening Gaza buffer zone as a part of a strategy to permanently displace them, pointing to remarks from senior ministers, including defense chief Israel Katz, saying they want to encourage “voluntary migration” from Gaza.

Netanyahu’s directive comes as Israel escalates its attacks in Gaza that it says target senior Hamas leaders who were involved in the 2023 attacks. On Tuesday, Israel killed Hamas’ armed wing chief, ten days after killing his predecessor.

Advertisement

Gaza health officials say an additional strike on Wednesday night that Israel said targeted two Hamas leaders had killed at least 10 people, including five children, and wounded 18 others.

That strike came as Palestinians were marking the Muslim holiday festival of Eid al-Adha, which many in Gaza celebrated by gathering together in tent encampments and in bombed-out buildings.

Advertisement

Etidal Al-Za’im said she was with her family inside their tent marking the holiday when suddenly rubble from the strike targeting the building next to them fell on top of them.

“We came out to the sound of a bang, we sat for an hour before we could come out through the (rubble) and find a way out of the tent,” she said.

Israeli strikes have killed more than 900 people since the truce, health officials say, while Israel says four soldiers were killed by militants during the same period. Israel and Hamas remain deadlocked in talks to advance a U.S. plan for Gaza that would see Israeli troops withdraw and Hamas disarm.

Advertisement

Another man who witnessed Wednesday’s Israeli strike, who identified himself as Abu Azam, said a “person in Gaza has no safety at all.”

“He could be hit in the street, he could be hit in the house, he could be hit in the hospital, he could be hit on his way to the market,” Abu Azam said.

Related
Israel gazaBenjamin NetanyahuHamas
